title: Streamlit UI — Layout, Model Selection & Prompt Input
goal: Build the core Streamlit application layout including the page configuration, sidebar with model selection dropdown (1–5 deployments), prompt text input area, and submit button. Wire the UI to config loading, client initialization, and deployment discovery on startup.

in_scope: Configure Streamlit page title, layout (wide), and icon
Display startup status: credential validation and deployment discovery
Show clear error messages in the UI if env vars are missing or discovery fails
Render a multi-select dropdown in the sidebar populated with discovered deployments
Enforce selection limit of 1–5 deployments with user-facing warnings
Provide a text area for prompt input
Provide a Submit button that triggers inference (actual execution wired in Part 07)
Use Streamlit session state to persist deployments, selections, and results across reruns
out_of_scope: Inference execution logic (Part 05 — called from UI but not implemented here)
Styling or theming beyond default Streamlit

inputs: PRD user stories GH-003, GH-004, GH-010, GH-011
outputs: src/app.py — updated with full UI layout, sidebar, model selection, and prompt input
dependencies: Part 02 — config.py and client.py modules exist
checklist: Set Streamlit page config: title='Azure Foundry LLM Arena', layout='wide'
Display the app title as an h1 header
On first run, call load_config() and store result in st.session_state
If load_config() raises ValueError, display st.error() with the exception message and st.stop()
On first run, call create_client() with the config and store client in st.session_state
On first run, call discover_deployments() and store results in st.session_state
If deployment discovery fails, display st.error() with the error message and st.stop()
If zero deployments are discovered, display st.warning() explaining no compatible deployments were found
In the sidebar, render st.multiselect() populated with deployment names from session state
If more than 5 deployments are selected, display st.warning() and disable the submit button
Below the sidebar, render st.text_area() for prompt input
Render a Submit button; disable it if no deployments are selected or prompt is empty
On submit, store the prompt and selected deployments in session state for use by Part 07
acceptance_criteria: The app launches with the correct title and wide layout
Missing env vars show a descriptive error and the app stops without crashing
Failed discovery shows an error message and the app stops gracefully
Zero deployments show a warning message; the dropdown is empty but the app does not crash
The multi-select dropdown displays all discovered deployment names
Selecting more than 5 deployments triggers a warning and prevents submission
The Submit button is disabled when no deployments are selected or prompt is empty
Session state correctly persists config, client, deployments, and selections across Streamlit reruns
review_focus: Session state initialization uses the 'if key not in st.session_state' pattern to avoid re-execution
Error display uses st.error() for fatal issues and st.warning() for non-fatal issues
The sidebar layout is clean and logically ordered: title → model selection → prompt → submit
Streamlit caching (@st.cache_resource or @st.cache_data) is used appropriately for client and discovery
The multi-select widget correctly maps deployment names to deployment metadata
risks_or_notes: Streamlit reruns the entire script on every interaction — session state management is critical
The SDK client should be cached with @st.cache_resource to avoid re-initialization on each rerun
Deployment discovery should ideally be cached to avoid repeated API calls
Consider adding a 'Refresh Deployments' button for cases where new deployments are added while the app is running
definition_of_done: src/app.py contains complete UI layout with sidebar, model selection, and prompt input
Environment variable errors are displayed in UI and stop execution
Discovery errors and empty results are handled gracefully in the UI
Submit button correctly stores prompt and selections in session state
App runs without errors when proper env vars are set and Foundry resource is accessible
